title: Tesla sales increase by 25% in Q2 2026
gist: Tesla delivered 480,126 EVs in Q2 2026, a 25% year-over-year increase driven largely by Europe, while intentionally producing ~30,000 fewer cars than it sold to draw down excess inventory; energy storage deployments rose 40% to 13.5 GWh.

insights.claim: Tesla sold 480,126 EVs in Q2 2026, a 25 percent year-over-year increase, with Model 3 and Y accounting for 467,762 deliveries (up 25.2 percent).
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: A 25% YoY delivery rebound signals demand recovery; the near-total reliance on two models is a concentration risk.
insights.locator: In total, Tesla sold 480,126 EVs during Q2

insights.claim: The remaining 12,364 non-3/Y vehicles (up 19 percent) were a mix of discontinued Models S and X and the Cybertruck, which is only sold in North America and the Middle East.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Tesla's premium/niche lineup is being pruned; Cybertruck's limited geographic sale caps its volume contribution.

insights.claim: Much of Tesla's Q2 sales surge came from Europe according to registration data, even though Tesla does not break out sales by geographic region.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: European demand is the swing factor behind the beat—watch European registration data as a leading indicator.
insights.locator: Although Tesla does not break out sales by geographic region

insights.claim: Tesla produced 451,758 cars in Q2 2026, nearly 30,000 fewer than it delivered, deliberately drawing down the inventory glut that had plagued it after building more cars than it could sell.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Producing below deliveries signals improving inventory discipline and potential margin support; a positive operational signal.
insights.locator: Better news for Tesla fans is that the company appears

insights.claim: Production of 'other' models fell 35 percent year-over-year to just 8,822 vehicles, reflecting the effect of the Model S and X cancellations.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Confirms Tesla is winding down low-volume premium models, narrowing the product base further.
insights.locator: Of those, 442,936 were Models 3 and Y

insights.claim: Tesla deployed 13.5 GWh of energy storage in Q2 2026, a 40 percent increase versus Q2 2025, outpacing its automotive growth rate.
insights.speaker: Jonathan M. Gitlin
insights.so_what: Energy storage growing faster than vehicles supports the thesis that stationary storage is an increasingly material Tesla segment.
insights.locator: Tesla’s energy storage sales aren’t looking too shabby
